summary: Authoritative national inventory: exploration status, localities, and reported grades/reserves for metallic and industrial minerals.
key_findings: Glass sand at Diimu, Malembo, Bukakata; DGSM cites up to 99.95% SiO₂ at Kome and 99.93% at Diimu/Bukakata (government figures — require independent assay for thesis tables).
Iron ore, phosphate (Sukulu), tin, gold, and niobium/tantalite listed with district-level occurrences.
limitations: Reserve figures are national survey estimates, not bankable feasibility studies.
use_in_thesis: Chapter 2 — Uganda resource endowment; Table of localities.

summary: BGS industrial mineral assessment with laboratory analyses for Diimu beach sand sample.
key_findings: Sample ~99.7% SiO₂; grains mostly 125–500 µm; Mukinda (1973) cited as finding deposit 'generally too coarse' for glass — grain size matters as much as chemistry.
limitations: Single sample; historical processing technology has changed.
use_in_thesis: Methods section — why grain-size distribution must be re-sampled.

summary: Field-based development minerals baseline across 22 districts; value chain focus on sand, stone, clay, dimension stone.
key_findings: Hundreds of active/abandoned sand and aggregate sites; formalization and OHS gaps.
Masaka region sand mining linked to construction demand, not integrated glass industry.
limitations: 2017 field season; policy landscape updated by 2022 Act.
use_in_thesis: Socio-economic chapter; contrast glass-grade vs construction sand markets.

summary: Makerere dissertation: GIS/remote sensing of wetland loss linked to sand mining in Lwera (Lake Victoria basin), 2010–2019.
key_findings: Sand mining footprint expanded; wetland patch metrics degraded over study period.
Lwera is in greater Masaka — relevant to lacustrine governance stream, not only Diimu.
limitations: Thesis — cite as grey literature; complement with NEMA policy documents.

summary: Peer-reviewed sediment contamination and health-risk framing at Port Bell (Lake Victoria, Uganda).
key_findings: Discusses dredging/lake sand mining in context of heavy metals in sediments.
Ecological risk indices flagged for some elements (e.g. Cd) — site-specific.
limitations: Port Bell ≠ Diimu; do not generalize chemistry across the lake.

summary: Corporate history: Kempen canal discovery (1860s), SCR founded 1872, Emsens land acquisitions in Lommel.
key_findings: Value chain began with local glass/crystal makers before export scale-up.
Mechanization and mergers consolidated Campine operators into modern Sibelco.
limitations: Corporate narrative — cross-check with independent environmental history.

summary: NGO description of post-mining nature reserve (heathland, forests, lakes).
key_findings: Demonstrates long-horizon ecological restoration as public amenity.
limitations: Management outcomes ≠ automatic template for tropical wetlands.

summary: Global floating solar outlook: cooling, land use, ecological considerations.
key_findings: Supports thermodynamic argument for water-cooled PV on suitable water bodies.
